title: Daily Market Digest
subtitle: April 07, 2026 - Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Markets

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Decline as Iran Deadline Looms and Ceasefire Hopes Fade
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are largely in the red today as investors react to escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and President Trump's deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Hopes for a ceasefire are fading, leading to a cautious market sentiment and notable declines across major indices. Oil prices have surged amidst the uncertainty.

items.statContext: Down 0.99% (66.46 points) in early trade today.
Down 1.45% (326.15 points) in early trade today.
Down 0.88% (408.87 points) in early trade today.

items.summary: UnitedHealth Group Inc. jumped after the Trump administration finalized a better-than-feared Medicare Advantage payment rate.

items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions Drive Market Uncertainty and Oil Price Surge
items.summary: Markets are highly sensitive to the unfolding situation in the Middle East. President Trump's ultimatum to Iran regarding the Strait of Hormuz has intensified fears, contributing to a risk-off sentiment. Crude oil prices have surged, with U.S. West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude trading near $115.40 per barrel. This geopolitical backdrop is overshadowing other market fundamentals.

items.headline: Technology Stocks Lag Amidst Broader Market Weakness, Energy Remains Volatile
items.summary: While Monday saw gains in consumer staples, consumer discretionary, and information technology sectors, today's trading indicates technology stocks are dragging down major indices. The energy sector continues to experience high volatility due to the geopolitical conflict. Goldman Sachs, however, suggests a 'generational opportunity' in tech stocks, noting valuations are lower than pre-2000 bubble levels.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data Released; Fed Expected to Hold Rates
items.summary: Today's economic releases offered a mixed picture. ADP's Weekly Employment Change showed its largest rise since inception, with private employers adding an average of 26,000 jobs per week in the four-week period ending March 21. However, U.S. durable goods orders fell 1.4% month-on-month in February, missing analysts' expectations. The ISM Manufacturing and Services PMIs for March were distorted by supply-chain disruptions, with services employment contracting sharply and price pressures surging. Markets are pricing in a 99.5% likelihood that the Federal Reserve will leave interest rates unchanged in its April meeting.

items.headline: Robust Earnings Season Anticipated Despite Headwinds
items.summary: Despite current market volatility and geopolitical concerns, the upcoming earnings season is expected to show robust performance in the U.S. market. JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon noted the U.S. economy remains resilient and businesses are healthy, though he cautioned on high asset prices.
